articles.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza killed 12, including four children
articles.summary: Israeli strikes on Gaza killed 12, including four children Gaza’s civil defence agency said that multiple Israeli strikes on several areas across Gaza have killed 12 Palestinians, including four children, on Tuesday. Witnesses reported that one air strike hit a house belonging to the al-Masri family in central Gaza City, which operates as a rescue service, killing six members of the family, including four children. articles.summary: UN condemns 'intensifying' Israeli attacks on Gaza The United Nations Office of the High Commissioner spokesperson, Thameen al-Kheetan, condemned on Tuesday the increased Israeli strikes on Gaza. The office recorded at least 57 Palestinians killed by Israel in Gaza between 13 and 20 July, including six children. “Nine months after the announcement of a ceasefire, still nowhere is safe for Palestinians in Gaza,” he added, noting that the killing of civilians raises concerns of war crimes. articles.title: US announces trade deal with Jordan
articles.summary: US announces trade deal with Jordan The US announced on Tuesday that they will be entering a trade deal with Jordan which would remove barriers for US exporters and provide new access for critical US industries.  According to a statement from the White House, Jordan will remove non-tariff trade barriers and expand market access for US goods, including agricultural products and motor vehicles, as part of the deal. This comes amid renewed Iranian strikes on Jordan, which killed two US military personnel on Friday.

The so-called Yellow Line, introduced on 10 October, became the main reference point for the areas where Israeli forces remained deployed.
